response.justification: Candidate brings 20+ years of broad full-stack and systems experience, strong open-source/Linux affinity, and founder-level ownership of a GIS platform, which aligns with the technical breadth and architecture expectations of the role. However, the position is explicitly a leadership/Head of Development role requiring C1 German and demonstrated team leadership, both of which are weak spots: German is listed as only Basic, and the resume shows senior IC work rather than explicit people-management or tech-lead headcount responsibility. Worth a screening call to probe leadership scope and German trajectory before committing.
strengths: Deep, polyglot engineering background across web, embedded, and distributed systems with strong open-source and Linux orientation
Founder of GISCollective demonstrates architectural ownership, Kubernetes deployment, and long-term product stewardship
Active community contributor (workshops, talks, courses) signaling mentoring aptitude relevant to leading a small team
concerns: German listed as only Basic — falls well short of the mandatory C1 requirement
No explicit people-management, hiring, or formal tech-lead role described; experience reads as senior IC rather than Head of Development
Python and UML — both called out in desired skills — are not visible in the skills list or project descriptions

key_factors.factor: Broad senior engineering and open-source/Linux experience
Limited evidence of formal leadership/management scope
